## [00:00 - 02:44]

Welcome to the Golden Rule. Why did you say it like that, Vin? Well, actually, I don't know. I just wanted this to be very dramatic. Because you know, in every industry, in every area of expertise, there's always these golden rules and sometimes they're cheesy, sometimes they're good, sometimes they're not that good. But I promise you, this one is very good. The Golden Rule when it comes to communication. This is a useful rule, especially when often I'll have my students ask me questions like, oh, Vin, I want to know, is my accent a problem when I communicate? Vin, I use a lot of ums and rs. Is that a problem, Vin? Vin, what about my movement? Am I moving around too much when I communicate? Am I moving my hands too much? So I get a bunch of questions like this and you can answer all of them by applying the Golden Rule. And the Golden Rule simply is, if it distracts people from the message, it's a problem. Let me say that again. If it distracts people from the message, it's a problem. If you're moving your hands too much while you're communicating and distracts people from the message, then yes, it is a problem. Now another one, for example, let me throw you to a video and let's see if you can identify what's distracting you from the message when you see one of my students communicate. So let's have a quick look. He gave us a good deal in regards to the minimum requirements to actually fulfill an order for these plastic bags. And we pursued the avenue of trying to find customers for these boutique bags. Why am I struggling? I'm not lifting anything. Everything you see here is done by video editing. It's done by the good old Craig Gibbons. But I just struggle anyway to make this video more engaging. With that example you just saw on video, what was distracting from the message? It was the ums and ars. It was the filler words. It was the non-words, the sounds that he was making to distract you from the message. So what that means in that particular situation is that the non-words and the filler words were a problem. It was distracting people from the message. Therefore, you've got to get rid of the non-words and the filler words or decrease them dramatically. So can you see how the golden rule is applied? Any time something is distracting people from the message, it's a problem. Now you know the epic golden rule in the world of communication and I hope it helps you on your journey.
